title: Debt Held by the Public
dataSource: Treasury Fiscal Data (Debt to the Penny)
context: The portion of the national debt owed to outside investors — individuals, funds, and foreign governments — as opposed to money the government owes itself (like the Social Security trust funds).
why: Economists usually treat debt held by the public as the most meaningful debt measure, because it reflects actual borrowing from financial markets and drives interest costs.
